ego: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eggon.
eloign: verb. To remove (something) to a distance.
eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.
enol: noun. An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group bonded to a carbon atom which is doubly bonded to another carbon atom.
enrol: verb. UK, New Zealand, Australia, Ireland, and Canada standard spelling of enroll.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sie.
gel: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fakkanci.
gen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Geman Deng.
genii: noun. plural of genius Guardian spirits.
genro: noun. A body of elder statesmen of Japan, formerly used as informal advisors to the Emperor.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
giro: noun. A transfer of funds between different account holders, carried out by the bank according to payer's written instructions.
glen: noun. A secluded and narrow valley, especially one with a river running through it; a depression between hills; a dale.
goer: noun. One who, or that which, goes.
goner: noun. Someone (or something) doomed; a hopeless case, especially someone who is bound to die soon.
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gore: noun. Blood, especially that from a wound when thickened due to exposure to the air.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
groin: noun. The crease or depression of the human body at the junction of the trunk and the thigh, together with the surrounding region.
ignore: verb. To deliberately not listen or pay attention to.
inlier: noun. An area of older rocks surrounded by younger rocks, typically formed by erosion of part of the younger rock.
inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
iring: verb. present participle and gerund of ire
iron: noun. A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a
irone: noun. Any of several ketones, or a mixture of such, found in orris oil (oil extracted from iris roots), used as odorants in perfumes.
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
lier: noun. A person or thing that lies, in the sense of being horizontal.
liger: noun. An animal born to a male lion and a tigress.
ligroin: noun. A petroleum fraction boiling 75°-125°C used as a solvent and fuel.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
line: noun. A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
liner: noun. Someone who fits a lining to something.
ling: noun. Any of various codlike saltwater fish often used as food by humans, those of the genus Molva, in the family Lotidae.
linger: verb. To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so.
lingo: noun. Language, especially language peculiar to a particular group, field, or region; jargon or a dialect.
logier: adjective. comparative form of logy: more logy
loin: noun. The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips.
loner: noun. One who is alone, lacking or avoiding the company of others.
long: adjective. Having much distance in space from one end to the other.
longe: verb. To work (a horse) in a circle at the end of a long line or rope.
longer: adjective. comparative form of long: more long
lore: noun. All the facts and traditions about a particular subject that have been accumulated over time through education or experience.
nerol: noun. The monoterpene alcohol (Z)-3,7-dimethyl-2,6-octadien-1-ol, found in many essential oils.
neroli: noun. More fully neroli oil or oil of neroli: an essential oil distilled from the blossoms of the bitter orange or Seville orange (Citrus ×
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
oiler: noun. One who or that which oils.
oilier: adjective. comparative form of oily: more oily
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.
olein: noun. Any naturally-occurring greasy or oily substance related to fat.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
oriel: noun. A large polygonal recess in a building, such as a bay window, forming a protrusion on the outer wall.
orle: noun. A bordure that runs around the outline of a shield without touching the edge.
reg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (Tanzania).
region: noun. Any considerable and connected part of a space or surface; specifically, a tract of land or sea of considerable but indefinite
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
reign: noun. The exercise of sovereign power.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
reoiling: verb. present participle and gerund of reoil
riel: noun. The monetary unit of Cambodia, equivalent to 100 sen or 10 kak.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rile: verb. To stir or move from a state of calm or order.

ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
